title: Dawferim
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Human/Legends|Human]] group inhabiting the [[Daupherm Planet States]]|the group of [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] known as the [[Dawferim Selfhood States]]|linebreak=1}} {{CulturalGroup |image=[[File:EvramDarkmere.png]] |name=Dawferim
{{C|The Dawferim Evram Darkmere (center) depicted}} |type= |leader= |subgroup= |origin= |region=[[Daupherm Planet States]], [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |language= |established= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |distinctions= |affiliation=Daupherm Planet States }} The '''Dawferim''', also known as the '''Dauferim''', were a [[Human/Legends|Human]] group that controlled more than thirty [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] in the [[Daupherm Planet States]] region of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. Since the early period of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Dawferim engaged in at least forty wars against the [[Botori]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of the nearby [[Botor Enclave/Legends|Botor Enclave]], with some of the conflicts lasting for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]]. Around [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], a [[Evram Darkmere's mother|pirate]] perished in one of the wars, leaving their ten-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old Dawferim [[Gender/Legends|son]], [[Evram Darkmere]], to raise both himself and his younger brother, [[Garreck Darkmere|Garreck]]. Over the following years, Darkmere went on to become a member of the [[Daupherm Merchant Space Fleet]] and the [[Daupherm Marine Corps]], after which he began a career as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]]. In approximately [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the Daupherm Planet States declared their independence from both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. ==Description== The Dawferim,''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' also referred to as the Dauferim, were a [[Human/Legends|Human]] group controlling more than thirty [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]—including the [[Daupherm system/Legends|Daupherm system]]—in the [[Daupherm Planet States]], a region of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. They shared their name with the group of systems known as the [[Dawferim Selfhood States]]. The [[Daupherm Systems Fleet]], the [[Daupherm Marine Corps]], and the [[Daupherm Merchant Space Fleet]] were all active in the Daupherm Planet States, and there was also at least one orphanage there.''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' ==History== ===Wars never-ending=== [[File:Daupherm_Planet_States_-_Botor_Enclave.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Dawferim inhabited the Daupherm Planet States and constantly conflicted with the Botor Enclave.]] During the early period of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Daupherm Planet States accepted the status of an [[Allied Region]] of that government, and since that [[Time/Legends|time]], the Dawferim and their ancient rivals, the [[Botori]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of the nearby [[Botor Enclave/Legends|Botor Enclave]], engaged in at least forty wars, with some of them lasting for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]]. A large number of laws and traditions regulating nearly every aspect of the conflicts, including vendettas, [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]], and all-out war, emerged over the subsequent [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]]. The Dawferim [[Gender/Legends|boy]] [[Evram Darkmere]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] to a female [[Evram Darkmere's mother|pirate]] around [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]].''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', which is set shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], establishes that [[Evram Darkmere]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] forty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, which corresponds to around [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. When Darkmere was ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old, his mother [[Death/Legends|died]] during one of the long wars between the Daupherm Planet States and the Botor Enclave. Darkmere was left having to raise both himself and his younger brother, [[Garreck Darkmere|Garreck]]. Two years later, Darkmere joined the Daupherm Merchant Space Fleet as a cabin boy—while his brother was placed in an orphanage. The older Dawferim was later drafted into the Daupherm Marine Corps, where he spent five years on a tour. ===Time of the Empire=== {{Quote|Darkmere is a dangerous character who isn't trustworthy by any means, but has been of great use to the Alliance.|Personal addendum by General Airen Cracken to an entry of a datafile detailing Rebel Alliance operatives dedicated to Evram Darkmere|}} [[File:DiscrilDiscril.png|thumb|right|300px|The ''Discril''-class attack cruiser stolen by Gorvan Shrulldike from the Daupherm Marine Corps]] After the tour's end, Darkmere joined [[Gorvan Shrulldike]], his former [[Captain/Legends|captain]], who stole their [[Retribution (Discril-class)|''Discril''-class attack cruiser]] in order to become a pirate. Several years later, Darkmere himself became the captain of the [[Attack cruiser/Legends|attack cruiser]]—with his pirate crew including old friends from the time he spent in the Daupherm Merchant Space Fleet—as well as a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] informant. At some point by the time shortly following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] included an entry on Darkmere to a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives. By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],The ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' is set six [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which corresponds to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. the Dawferim and the Botor Enclave had engaged in a five-hundred-year-long small-scale [[Daupherm-Boton Conflict|feud]]. Around that same time, the Daupherm Planet States also declared their independence from both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Dawferim were first mentioned in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', [[Michael Allen Horne]]'s [[1993]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. [[Mike Jackson]] illustrated the Dawferim Evram Darkmere for the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', while the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] introduced the alternate name "Dauferim" for the group. ==Sources== {{Indexpage}} *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' {{1stp}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{1stID|Dauferim}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Human cultural groups]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Dawferim]] }}
